rules.description: Governance review wins when the request concerns approval, rollback, safety policy, evolution policy, controlled release, audit, or high-risk truth tracking.
Clarification wins when target, permission, state scope, required files, destructive risk, or acceptance criteria are missing.
Roundtable wins over autonomous_agent for explicit RTCM/roundtable/debate/meeting requests, high-stakes architecture or strategy decisions, multi-party dissent, or irreversible governance decisions.
Roundtable must not steal clear low-risk execution, bounded search, direct answer, or task requests unless the user explicitly asks for discussion or the governance layer requires review.
If roundtable is only inferred from complexity or uncertainty, request human confirmation before activating RTCM.
Workflow wins over task when the work is reusable, recurring, multi-system, DAG-like, has checkpoints, or needs orchestration policy.
Task wins over workflow for bounded one-off work with a clear target and no reusable process requirement.
Search wins when the answer depends on current, external, cited, or verifiable facts.
Direct answer is allowed only for stable, short, no-tool, no-state-change requests.
User explicit mode hints win over automatic classification unless blocked by safety, governance, missing context, or impossible execution permissions.
Mixed mode is not a raw fallback; it must decompose into governed child ModeDecision records with explicit result sinks.
